title: Protocol of Cross Meta-Analysis on bioproducts extraction - regional and uncertainties evaluation: case study of Policosanol to PTCVD and Ibero-American
author: PRIMO, Rilton Gonçalo
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This protocol of Cross Meta-Analyses (P-CMA) of articles and patents can be used for biorefining methods or technological routes for  extracting bioproducts in general, as well as for any region or set of countries. The case of policosanols (PC), as substances indicated  for the prevention and treatment of cardiovascular diseases (PTCVD), combined with the case of Ibero-American countries, is a  demonstration, considering this gap in the literature, among other existing ones. This P-CMA was designed for comparisons between  two or more routes/regions, including different bioproducts/industrial outputs. Closely following initiatives such as International  Registry for Prospective Reviews and Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses, in order to avoid arbitrary  revisions, rework and encourage cooperation, this P-CMA integrates an explicit set of procedures and tools, in a sequence of 11  sequential processes. While recommending the use of platforms, search engines and databases as broad as those made available by the CAPES Periodicals Portal, Orbit Intelligence, SciFinder, Scopus and Web of Science - Core Collection (Clarivate Analytics), the  comparison and supplementation of a base class by other(s) was an identified need. The results of applying this P-CMA will be  disseminated through a peer-reviewed journal, in the form of 1) lists of the most efficient routes of policosanol extraction, which can  be hierarchical, case by case, according to the availability of raw materials, inputs and technology, 2) regionalized identification of  gaps, perspectives, and networks of scientific and technological cooperation, 3) review of physical productivities with respective  assessments of uncertainty.

keywords: Cross Meta-Analysis of Articles and Patents
Six main routes of Policosanol extraction
Prevention and treatment of cardiovascular diseases
